title: Lemon Herb Baked Salmon
description: Flaky salmon fillet with lemon, chives and fresh herbs. Ready in 25 minutes and packed with omega-3s.

steps.instruction: Preheat oven to 200°C / 180°C fan / 400°F. Line a baking dish with parchment paper.
Place salmon fillets skin-side down in the baking dish.
Whisk together olive oil, lemon juice, parsley, chives, salt and pepper. Pour over the salmon.
Bake for 18–20 minutes until the salmon flakes easily with a fork.
Serve immediately with steamed rice and a simple green salad.

title: Overnight Oats with Blueberries
description: Creamy no-cook oats that you prep the night before. Topped with fresh blueberries and maple syrup.

steps.instruction: Combine oats, almond milk, Greek yoghurt, maple syrup and chia seeds in a jar or container. Stir well.
Cover and refrigerate overnight, or for at least 4 hours.
In the morning, stir the oats. If too thick, add a splash more almond milk.
Top with fresh blueberries and serve cold.

title: Chicken Rice Bowl with Chive Sauce
description: Juicy grilled chicken over fluffy white rice with a tangy chive and Greek yoghurt sauce.

steps.instruction: Cook rice according to packet instructions (typically 12 minutes boiling).
Season chicken with salt, pepper and olive oil. Cook in a skillet over medium-high heat for 6–7 minutes each side until cooked through.
Meanwhile, mix Greek yoghurt, chives and lemon juice in a small bowl. Season with salt.
Slice chicken and serve over rice with chive sauce drizzled on top.

title: Scrambled Eggs with Spinach & Tomato
description: Soft, creamy scrambled eggs with wilted baby spinach and juicy cherry tomatoes. A FODMAP-safe breakfast classic.

steps.instruction: Whisk eggs with almond milk, salt and pepper in a bowl.
Melt butter in a non-stick pan over low-medium heat. Add cherry tomatoes and cook for 2 minutes until softened.
Add spinach and stir until wilted, about 1 minute.
Pour in egg mixture. Using a spatula, gently fold the eggs as they cook. Remove from heat while still slightly soft — residual heat will finish them.
Serve immediately on gluten-free toast if desired.

title: Tuna & Rice Salad
description: A quick, filling lunch bowl with canned tuna, fluffy rice, cucumber, carrot and a simple lemon dressing.

steps.instruction: Cook rice, then spread on a tray to cool quickly.
Whisk together olive oil, lemon juice, mustard, salt and pepper.
Combine cooled rice, drained tuna, cucumber, carrot and spring onion greens in a bowl.
Drizzle over dressing and toss gently to combine. Adjust seasoning and serve.

title: Beef Mince Stir-Fry with Vegetables
description: Quick weeknight stir-fry with lean beef mince, colourful capsicum, bok choy and tamari sauce over rice.

steps.instruction: Cook rice. While rice cooks, prep all vegetables.
Heat garlic-infused oil in a wok or large skillet over high heat. Add beef mince and cook, breaking up, for 5 minutes until browned.
Add ginger, carrot and capsicum. Stir-fry for 3 minutes.
Add bok choy and tamari. Toss everything together and cook for 2 more minutes.
Serve over rice, garnished with spring onion greens.

title: Banana Pancakes (2-Ingredient)
description: Fluffy, naturally sweet pancakes made with just banana and eggs. No flour, no fuss.

steps.instruction: Mash banana well with a fork in a bowl until very smooth.
Add eggs and whisk together with the banana.
Melt butter in a non-stick pan over medium-low heat.
Pour in small rounds of batter (about 2 tbsp each). Cook for 2–3 minutes until bubbles appear on the surface, then flip and cook 1 minute more.
Serve immediately with maple syrup and fresh strawberries if desired.

title: Quinoa Salad with Feta & Roasted Vegetables
description: Hearty quinoa salad with roasted capsicum, zucchini, baby spinach and crumbled feta. Great for meal prep.

steps.instruction: Preheat oven to 200°C. Toss capsicum and zucchini in 1 tbsp olive oil, salt and pepper. Roast for 20–25 minutes until golden.
Rinse quinoa well, then cook in 400ml water with a pinch of salt for 12 minutes. Fluff with a fork and cool slightly.
Whisk together remaining olive oil, lemon juice, salt and pepper.
Combine quinoa, roasted vegetables, baby spinach and cherry tomatoes. Drizzle with dressing and toss.
Top with crumbled feta. Serve warm or at room temperature. Keeps in fridge for 3 days.

title: Herb Chicken Skewers
description: Marinated chicken thigh skewers with fresh herbs and lemon. Perfect for grilling or baking.

steps.instruction: Cut chicken thighs into 3cm cubes. Thread onto metal or pre-soaked bamboo skewers.
Mix together garlic-infused oil, lemon juice, rosemary, oregano, turmeric, salt and pepper.
Pour marinade over skewers and set aside for 15 minutes (or overnight in fridge).
Grill or bake at 220°C for 18–20 minutes, turning halfway, until cooked through and charred.
Serve with rice, roasted vegetables or a simple salad.

title: Peanut Butter Banana Smoothie
description: A thick, creamy breakfast smoothie with banana, peanut butter and almond milk. Ready in 2 minutes.

steps.instruction: Add all ingredients to a blender.
Blend on high for 60 seconds until completely smooth.
Pour into a glass and serve immediately.

title: Baked Chicken Thighs with Roasted Carrots
description: Simple one-tray dinner with golden baked chicken thighs and sweet roasted carrots.

steps.instruction: Preheat oven to 200°C / 180°C fan.
Mix olive oil, paprika, cumin, salt and pepper. Toss carrots in half the mixture and spread on a baking tray.
Coat chicken thighs with remaining spice oil. Place on top of or alongside the carrots.
Bake for 35–40 minutes, until chicken skin is golden and the juices run clear.
Scatter with fresh parsley and serve with steamed rice or quinoa.

title: Rice Noodle Soup with Chicken
description: Light, comforting noodle soup with tender chicken, bok choy and ginger in a simple broth.

steps.instruction: Bring 1.5 litres of water to the boil with ginger slices. Simmer 5 minutes for flavour.
Add chicken slices and poach for 6–8 minutes until cooked through.
Soak rice noodles in boiling water in a separate bowl for 5 minutes, drain.
Add fish sauce and tamari to the broth. Taste and adjust seasoning.
Add bok choy to the broth for the last 2 minutes. Divide noodles between bowls, ladle over soup and top with spring onion greens and herbs.

title: Egg Fried Rice
description: Classic fried rice using day-old rice, scrambled eggs, carrots and spring onion tops. Better than takeaway.

steps.instruction: Heat garlic-infused oil in a wok over very high heat until smoking.
Add carrot and stir-fry for 2 minutes.
Push everything to the side, crack in the eggs and scramble quickly.
Add cold rice and toss everything together, breaking up any clumps.
Add tamari and pepper. Toss for 2 minutes until everything is hot and well combined. Add spring onion tops and serve.

title: Greek Salad with Feta
description: Simple Mediterranean salad with cucumber, tomato, olives and feta. No onion, FODMAP-safe.

steps.instruction: Chop cucumber, tomatoes and capsicum into similar-sized cubes.
Whisk olive oil, vinegar, oregano, salt and pepper.
Combine vegetables and olives in a bowl. Drizzle with dressing.
Top with feta and serve immediately or refrigerate for up to 2 hours.

title: Strawberry Chia Pudding
description: Creamy chia seed pudding with fresh strawberries and vanilla. Prep the night before for a grab-and-go breakfast.

steps.instruction: Whisk together chia seeds, almond milk, maple syrup and vanilla.
Let sit for 5 minutes, then stir again to prevent clumping.
Cover and refrigerate overnight or for at least 4 hours.
Stir before serving. Divide into bowls and top with sliced strawberries.

title: Polenta with Sautéed Zucchini & Feta
description: Creamy soft polenta topped with garlicky (infused oil) sautéed zucchini and crumbled feta.

steps.instruction: Bring 600ml lightly salted water to a boil. Whisk in polenta and reduce heat. Stir for 5–7 minutes until thick and creamy. Season and keep warm.
Heat garlic-infused oil in a pan over medium-high heat. Add zucchini in a single layer and cook without moving for 3 minutes until golden.
Flip zucchini, season with salt and pepper, and cook another 2 minutes.
Spoon polenta into bowls, top with zucchini, crumbled feta and spring onion greens.

title: Tempeh & Vegetable Stir-Fry
description: Protein-packed vegan stir-fry with tempeh, bok choy, carrots and a ginger-tamari sauce.

steps.instruction: Cook rice. Whisk tamari, ginger and maple syrup in a small bowl.
Heat oil in a wok over high heat. Add tempeh and fry for 4–5 minutes until golden on all sides.
Add carrot and stir-fry 2 minutes. Add bok choy and toss for 2 more minutes.
Pour in the sauce, toss everything together for 1 minute and serve over rice.

title: Omelette with Spinach, Tomato & Chives
description: Fluffy classic omelette filled with baby spinach, cherry tomatoes and fresh chives.

steps.instruction: Whisk eggs with salt and a splash of water.
Melt butter in a 20cm non-stick pan over medium heat. Add tomatoes for 1 minute.
Pour in eggs. As edges set, use a spatula to push them toward the centre while tilting the pan.
When mostly set but still glossy, add spinach and chives to one half. Fold omelette over and slide onto a plate.

title: Baked Salmon with Quinoa & Greens
description: Nutritious dinner with omega-3 salmon, fluffy quinoa and sautéed kale.

steps.instruction: Cook quinoa: rinse, then simmer in 280ml water for 12 minutes. Fluff and season.
Preheat oven to 200°C. Drizzle salmon with olive oil, lemon and seasoning. Bake 15–18 minutes.
Heat garlic-infused oil in a pan. Add kale with a splash of water and cover for 3–4 minutes until wilted. Season.
Plate quinoa, add kale alongside, and place salmon on top. Squeeze over extra lemon.

title: Rice Pudding with Maple & Strawberries
description: Comforting stovetop rice pudding sweetened with maple syrup and topped with fresh strawberries.

steps.instruction: Combine rice and milk in a saucepan over medium heat. Bring to a gentle simmer, stirring frequently.
Reduce heat to low. Cook for 20–25 minutes, stirring often, until rice is tender and pudding is creamy.
Remove from heat. Stir in maple syrup, cinnamon and vanilla.
Serve warm or cold, topped with sliced strawberries.

title: Pork Tenderloin with Roasted Capsicum
description: Juicy pork tenderloin with sweet roasted red capsicum and fresh herbs. Elegant and IBS-friendly.

steps.instruction: Preheat oven to 200°C. Toss capsicum in 1 tbsp garlic oil, season and place in a roasting tin.
Rub pork with remaining oil, salt, pepper and rosemary.
Sear pork in an oven-safe pan over high heat for 3 minutes each side until golden.
Transfer to the oven alongside capsicum. Roast 20–25 minutes until pork reaches 65°C internal temperature. Rest 5 minutes.
Slice pork and serve with roasted capsicum and a squeeze of lemon.

title: Corn Tortilla Tacos with Chicken
description: Crispy corn tortilla tacos with seasoned chicken, lettuce, tomato and a squeeze of lime.

steps.instruction: Season chicken with paprika, cumin and salt.
Heat garlic-infused oil in a skillet over high heat. Cook chicken 5–6 minutes until cooked through and slightly charred. Rest and slice.
Warm tortillas in a dry pan or directly on a gas flame for 20 seconds each side.
Fill each tortilla with chicken, lettuce and tomato. Squeeze over lime juice and serve.

title: Blueberry Almond Milk Smoothie Bowl
description: Thick, spoonable smoothie bowl with frozen blueberries, banana and almond milk topped with seeds.

ingredients.notes: ¼ cup — just enough to blend

steps.instruction: Blend frozen blueberries, frozen banana and almond milk until very thick and smooth. Add more milk 1 tbsp at a time only if needed — keep it thick.
Pour into a bowl and top with chia seeds, pumpkin seeds and sliced strawberries. Serve immediately.

title: Prawn & Rice Noodle Salad
description: Light Asian-style cold noodle salad with prawns, cucumber, carrot, herbs and a zingy lime dressing.

steps.instruction: Soak rice noodles in boiling water for 5 minutes. Drain, rinse under cold water and set aside.
Whisk lime juice, fish sauce and sugar until sugar dissolves.
Use a vegetable peeler to make cucumber and carrot ribbons.
Combine noodles, vegetables, prawns and coriander. Pour over dressing and toss gently. Garnish with spring onion tops.

title: One-Pan Chicken & Potato Bake
description: Hearty all-in-one bake with chicken thighs, diced potatoes and cherry tomatoes — minimal washing up.

steps.instruction: Preheat oven to 210°C. Toss potatoes in garlic oil, oregano and half the salt. Spread in a large roasting tin.
Season chicken with remaining salt. Place on top of potatoes, skin side up.
Scatter cherry tomatoes and lemon slices around the chicken.
Roast for 45–50 minutes until chicken skin is golden and crisp and potatoes are cooked through. Serve straight from the tray.

title: Creamy Lactose-Free Pasta (GF)
description: Rich, creamy gluten-free pasta with a lactose-free cream sauce, parmesan and fresh chives.

steps.instruction: Cook gluten-free pasta in well-salted boiling water until al dente. Reserve ½ cup pasta water before draining.
In the empty pasta pot, warm garlic-infused oil over medium heat. Add cream and lactose-free milk. Simmer gently for 2 minutes.
Add drained pasta, parmesan and black pepper. Toss well, adding pasta water a splash at a time until sauce is silky.
Serve topped with chives and extra parmesan.

title: Millet Porridge with Berries
description: Warm, nutty millet porridge — a gluten-free oat alternative — with blueberries, maple and almond milk.

steps.instruction: Rinse millet under cold water. Combine with almond milk and cinnamon in a saucepan.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 15–20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until creamy and thick.
Stir in maple syrup. Add a splash more almond milk if too thick.
Serve topped with blueberries and a dollop of peanut butter.

title: Turkey Lettuce Wraps
description: Light, fresh turkey mince filling in crispy iceberg cups — a low-carb FODMAP-friendly lunch.

steps.instruction: Heat garlic-infused oil in a large pan over high heat. Add turkey mince and cook 8 minutes, breaking up, until browned.
Add ginger and tamari. Stir-fry for 2 minutes more.
Remove from heat. Stir in carrot and spring onion tops.
Separate iceberg lettuce leaves into cups. Spoon filling into each cup. Garnish with coriander and a squeeze of lime.

title: Dark Chocolate Bliss Balls
description: No-bake energy balls with oats, peanut butter, dark chocolate and maple syrup. 10 minutes, no oven.

steps.instruction: Combine all ingredients in a bowl. Mix well until everything comes together. If too dry, add 1 tsp more maple syrup.
Refrigerate for 15 minutes to firm slightly.
Roll into 12 balls (about 1 tbsp each). Store in the fridge for up to 1 week or freeze for 1 month.

title: Beef & Vegetable Soup
description: Warming, hearty soup with lean beef, carrots, potatoes and zucchini. Perfect batch-cook for the week.

steps.instruction: Heat garlic-infused oil in a large pot over high heat. Brown beef in batches (don't crowd the pan), about 3 minutes. Set aside.
Add tomato paste to the pot and cook for 1 minute, then add 1.5 litres of water, turmeric, cumin and salt.
Return beef and add carrots and potatoes. Bring to a boil, then reduce to a gentle simmer.
Simmer for 30 minutes until beef is tender. Add zucchini and cook a further 10 minutes.
Taste and adjust seasoning. Serve with fresh parsley. Keeps in fridge for 4 days; freezes well for 2 months.
